definition: The experience of Jim Pike "returning" to Dick is interpreted as the "reconstitution" of a personality, serving as proof of the truth of Christianity.
interpretive_note: Horselover Fat is Philip K. Dick's semi-autobiographical alter ego, most prominently featured as the protagonist of the novel *VALIS*. The name itself is a linguistic translation of Dick's own name: 'Philip' derives from Greek for 'lover of horses', and 'Dick' means 'fat' in German. In the Exegesis, 'Horselover Fat' frequently represents the fragmented, traumatized, and increasingly unhinged aspect of Dick's own psyche following the intense 2-3-74 visions. By splitting himself into two characters in the novel ('Philip K. Dick' the rational science fiction writer, and 'Horselover Fat' the obsessed, visionary madman), Dick attempted to analytically distance himself from his own overwhelming mystical experiences and the sprawling, often contradictory theology he was developing to explain them.
